title: PMM deep-link → per-engine Instance Summary dashboards + floating version badge
originDiscussion: v0.4.6's /inventory/services/<id> deep link returned nginx 404 in the browser — PMM 3 has no per-service-id SPA route. Operator also asked for a more visible version indicator beyond the small sidebar text.
rootCause: v0.4.6 fix dropped /graph prefix based on curl 401 vs 404 evidence, but PMM 3.x simply has no per-service-id UI URL at all. The actual deep target for 'see this service in PMM' is the per-engine Grafana Instance Summary dashboard, filtered via ?var-service_name=<name>.

scope.deepLinkRewrite: PMM_DASHBOARD_BY_ENGINE map: mysql/mariadb → mysql-instance-summary, postgresql → postgresql-instance-summary, mongodb → mongodb-instance-summary. Dashboard UIDs confirmed live against PMM 3.5.0 (/graph/api/search). For redis/mssql/unknown — engines without a shipped per-instance dashboard — fall back to PMM's Inventory landing page at /graph/a/pmm-app/inventory.
scope.versionBadge: Floating top-right badge in AppShell — absolute-positioned, pointer-events-none, mono small, survives sidebar collapse, present on every route. Source: Vite-injected __APP_VERSION__ from apps/web/package.json (single source of truth, replaces the hardcoded string in sidebar.tsx that needed bumping every release).

futureReleaseProcessChange: From v0.4.7 onwards, releases bump 4 package.json files only. The web sidebar + floating badge both read APP_VERSION from @/lib/version (Vite-injected at build time). No more separate sidebar.tsx string to keep in sync.
